title: Stash Internals, Partial Work, and Portable Patches · Recovery Case: Gita Stash Object Model
summary: An urgent repair interrupts mixed staged, unstaged, untracked, and ignored experiments; some work must remain visible, some move across clones, and no secret may enter an object. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita stash object model increment in work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
Verify source provenance, target applicability, conflicts, changed-platform behavior, release notes, signatures, and rollback for each supported line.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Stash Object Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G29 is a theory in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Stash Object Model contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Stash Object Model while producing work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G29 is a worked example in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. Competency probe: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag. A defensible solution applies Gita Stash Object Model, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-stash-object-model — Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible
git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip
# expected: the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths
# observation: policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection
# changed-case: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash
# transfer-limit: inspect real ignored and untracked paths because stash can store sensitive bytes in Git objects
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Stash Object Model evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-stash-object-model-1idmka" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Stash Object Model into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-stash-object-model-1idmka
# scenario-key: trace-1idmka probe-akmdi1 boundary-dmka1i owner-1idmkax fault-1idmkaz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1i candidate=b00b1ka
# hypothesis: only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible
git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip
# expected: the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths
# observation: policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection
# changed-case: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes
# after-ref: main=a11ce1i candidate=c0ffeea
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash
# transfer-limit: inspect real ignored and untracked paths because stash can store sensitive bytes in Git objects

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
